etymology.description: semantic 大(大) (dà, "big; great") + phonetic 可(可) (kě) -> strange; odd; unique; rare
etymology.interpretation: The character 奇(qí) combines 大(大) (dà, "big; great") as the semantic component indicating something substantial or significant, with 可(可) (kě) providing the phonetic element. Together, this combination suggests something that stands out as significant due to its unusual or unique nature, leading to meanings such as 'strange', 'odd', 'unique', and 'rare'.

examples.chinese: 这是一场奇怪的梦(這是一場奇怪的夢)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè shì yī chǎng qíguài de mèng.

examples.chinese: 他有一种奇异的天赋(他有一種奇異的天賦)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yǒu yī zhǒng qíyì de tiānfù.

examples.chinese: 这本书中包含许多奇闻异事(這本書中包含許多奇聞異事)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè běn shū zhōng bāohán xǔduō qíwén yìshì.
examples.english: This book contains many strange tales.
